title: 161 Combat Engagements Recorded March 21; Russia Deploys 8,107 Drones
detail: Ukraine's General Staff recorded 161 combat engagements on March 21, 2026. Russia launched 82 airstrikes and deployed 8,107 kamikaze drones in one day. Russian losses for the day: 1,240 personnel killed or wounded, plus losses in armor, artillery, and vehicles. Ukraine successfully repelled 7 Russian advancement attempts in multiple sectors including Rai-Oleksandrivka, Yampil, and Platonivka.

title: Zelenskyy Demands Timeline for Peace Negotiations
detail: President Zelenskyy on March 20 called for resumption of peace talks with Russia, demanding 'clear dates—at least approximate ones' for negotiations. The main sticking point remains Russia's demand for Ukrainian surrender of the remaining 20% of Donetsk region, which Ukraine refuses without binding security guarantees. Peace talks have been paused due to the US-Israeli military operations against Iran dominating Western diplomatic attention.

title: Ukraine Power Generation Collapsed to 14 GW — 58% Below Pre-War Levels
detail: Ukraine's power generation capacity has collapsed from 33.7 GW at the start of the invasion to just 14 GW as of January 2026 — a 58% reduction. Systematic Russian targeting of energy infrastructure has degraded civilian capacity severely. Russia's drone capabilities have also escalated dramatically, with December 2025 drone launches tripling compared to December 2024. New Molniya-2 Starlink-enabled drones are extending attack ranges beyond 230 km.
